description:
Offered: every fourth semester
The course will stress the development of what is a conspicuously American type of humor. The course begins with the quintessential American humorist and satirist, Mark Twain and then explores a range of comic genres and themes, from black comedy to feminist humor, that continue to enliven our national popular culture in many media. This course explores the writings and career of Mark Twain, and humorists who share the Twain legacy, including American film comedy and stand-up comics.
Prerequisite(s): ENGL 1200 or HON 1200
Meets General Education Requirement(s): AIA, IHIP, LI
